summary: The child shows age-appropriate developmental milestones in terms of timing, but exhibits significant motor impairments affecting both upper and lower limbs, specifically hemiplegic patterns with asymmetry.
gross_motor_level: Achieved milestones like crawling, walking, and climbing, but presents with significant gait abnormalities including toe-walking, in-toeing, out-toeing, and foot dropping.
fine_motor_level: Capable of grasping, stacking, and self-feeding, but suffers from significant impairments in bilateral coordination, pincer grasp, grip strength, and finger control.
impairment_severity: Moderate; presence of hemiplegic-style impairments (asymmetry in arm and leg) involving toe-walking, foot drop, thumb-in-palm posture, and pronated forearm.
supporting_evidence: Achieved milestones like crawling and walking within normal timeframes | Presence of toe-walking, in-toeing, and out-toeing | Presence of foot dropping (difficulty lifting the front part of the foot) | Upper limb impairments including thumb-in-palm posture and pronated forearm | Difficulty with bilateral coordination and finger control | Report of persistent impact on the right arm and leg with asymmetry

summary: The child shows age-appropriate milestone achievement in gross and fine motor skills despite significant neurological impairments in both upper and lower limbs.
gross_motor_level: Functional milestones achieved (squatting, standing), but significant gait abnormalities including toe-walking, in-toeing, out-toeing, and foot drop.
fine_motor_level: Good functional use of hands (scribbling, stacking, utensil use), but significant impairments in dexterity, grip, coordination, and finger control.
impairment_severity: Moderate; presence of multi-focal impairments in both upper and lower extremities causing asymmetry and gait deviations.
supporting_evidence: Achieved age-appropriate milestones for a 2-year-old | Squats to pick up objects and stands back up | Uses fork and spoon with little spilling | Significant lower limb impairments: Toe-walking, In-toeing, Out-toering, and Dropping foot | Significant upper limb impairments: Difficulty with pincer grasp, bilateral coordination, and finger control | Presence of asymmetry affecting legs and the right hand | Progressive increase in strength and stability noted

summary: The child shows age-appropriate gross motor milestones like pedaling a tricycle and skating, but exhibits noticeable gait asymmetries and lower limb impairments. Upper limb function shows mild coordination and dexterity difficulties, though progress in right-hand usage is noted.
gross_motor_level: Relatively good mobility with specific gait impairments including toe-walking, in-toeing, out-toeing, and foot drop.
fine_motor_level: Functional for age-related tasks like using a hockey stick, but shows difficulties with bilateral coordination, pinching, and finger control.
impairment_severity: Moderate; presence of toe-walking, foot drop, and gait asymmetry, alongside mild upper limb coordination and dexterity issues.
supporting_evidence: Pedals a tricycle and hops on one foot | Skates without help | Toe-walking (Equinus Gait) present | In-toeing and out-toeing present | Foot drop present | Difficulty with bilateral coordination and pinching | Gait asymmetry becomes more evident when running or increasing speed | Progress noted in right hand and arm use
